name: edge-case
task: Our application is hitting connection limits on Supabase Postgres. We have 200 serverless function instances each opening their own connection. The database shows 200+ active connections and is becoming unresponsive. How do we fix this?
assertions: Response recommends using connection pooling via PgBouncer or the Supabase connection pooler
Response explains the difference between transaction mode and session mode pooling
Response recommends connecting through the pooler endpoint (port 6543) instead of the direct connection (port 5432) or equivalent Supabase pooler URL
Response mentions that each Postgres connection consumes 1-3MB of RAM or otherwise explains why raw connection count is a resource problem
Response suggests configuring pool size based on available resources (e.g., `(CPU cores * 2) + spindle_count` formula or a concrete small number like 10-20 connections in the pool)
